nodes.title: Mathematical Foundations for Game Physics
nodes.rationale: A solid understanding of linear algebra, vector calculus, and quaternions is crucial for accurately representing and manipulating physical objects, forces, and rotations in a 3D environment. This module ensures a strong theoretical base for subsequent implementation.

nodes.title: Rigid Body Dynamics
nodes.rationale: Understanding how to represent and simulate the motion of rigid bodies under various forces and torques is fundamental. This module covers the theoretical underpinnings of rigid body motion, including mass, inertia, and angular momentum.

nodes.title: Numerical Integration Techniques
nodes.rationale: Accurately advancing the state of rigid bodies through time requires robust numerical integration schemes. This module explores various integrators and their stability/accuracy trade-offs, essential for a stable simulation.

nodes.title: Collision Detection Algorithms
nodes.rationale: Detecting when two or more objects intersect or come into contact is a primary function of a physics engine. This module covers various algorithms for efficiently identifying potential and actual collisions.

nodes.title: Broad-Phase Collision Detection
nodes.rationale: For scenes with many objects, checking every pair for collision is computationally expensive. Broad-phase algorithms efficiently narrow down the potential collision pairs, drastically improving performance.

nodes.title: Narrow-Phase Collision Detection
nodes.rationale: After broad-phase identifies potential pairs, narrow-phase algorithms perform precise intersection tests to determine exact contact points and normals. This is where detailed geometry interaction is handled.

nodes.title: Collision Response and Resolution
nodes.rationale: Once a collision is detected, the engine must calculate the appropriate forces or impulses to prevent interpenetration and simulate realistic bouncing or sliding. This module delves into methods for resolving detected collisions.

nodes.title: Constraint Solvers
nodes.rationale: Constraints are essential for simulating joints, hinges, and other limitations on rigid body motion. This module covers techniques for enforcing these constraints realistically and stably.

nodes.title: Physics Engine Architecture and Optimization
nodes.rationale: Designing a robust, performant, and extensible physics engine requires careful architectural considerations and optimization techniques. This module focuses on structuring the engine and improving its efficiency.
